So then I put my foot on the accelerator. i'm late again then you're gonna fire me so then i put my foot on the accelerator and i drove straight through all of them you drove through seven baby ducks including the mother oh that's the kind of commitment i'm talking about that's what gets you promoted here

descripcion: Here’s how to identify a bad apology.
transcripcion: Bad apologies are ones like, I'm sorry that you, I'm sorry that you feel that way. You're not sorry for anything. So how I would want you to respond to that is, don't apologize for my feelings. Apologize for what you did or apologize for what you said. Because that's really what they're saying. I'm sorry you feel that way. You don't need to apologize for my feelings. I'll never forget the first time I did that with a guy who was being very rude. I said, well, I'm sorry that you feel that way. And I said, oh, you're fine. You don't have to apologize for my feelings. Well, I'm sorry that you feel that way. And I said, oh, you're fine. You don't have to apologize for my feelings. What I need you to do is apologize for the tone you just gave me. Like that was, I mean, he couldn't do anything with that. And so you're removing the excuse. Same thing if somebody says like a conditional apologies. These are ones that say, well, I'm sorry if, well, I'm sorry if that upset you. I'm sorry if you didn't like that. Quick way to respond to that is it's not if it's that. Meaning, I'm sorry that. It's not if, it's that. Meaning, I'm sorry that you did X, Y, and Z. Instead of I'm sorry if that hurt you, it's I'm sorry that it hurt you.

bio:

descripcion:
8 ways to be a better communicator 🙌

Which top is your favorite?
transcripcion: Best way to improve your communication skills? Develop more self -awareness by looking into the communication mirror, which is what? Record a video of yourself for five minutes and then watch it back. What's a daily life hack to improve articulation? Grab a book, read it out loud, and overdo the mouth movements. Biggest mistake with hand gestures? Often people keep it in their pocket or they're fidgeting with it. Use your hands with intention and use them on purpose. How do you say no gracefully? No. What's your favorite World of Warcraft race? That's a stupid question, Peter. No. What's your favourite World of Warcraft race? That's a stupid question, Peter. Night elves. How do I stop sounding monotone? Practicing the siren technique, which is, again, grab a book, read it with a really low note, then gradually go high, high, high, and don't be afraid to go all the way up, gentlemen, and then come all the way back down. Do that for about five minutes each day. How do you be a better conversationalist? If you notice someone speaking about something that really lights them up, ask them three follow -up questions on the very thing they were talking about. How do I stop saying um and ah? Every single time you feel the urge. How do I stop saying um and ah? Every single time you feel the urge to say um or ah, replace it with a pause. You replace the habit you don't want with something that you do want.

descripcion: Comment '3V' if you want to learn the full self-awareness process to identify your non-functional communication habits!
transcripcion: What would be your top three tips for effective communication? Here's a three -step process. Number one, when you create content, you must watch it back. But when you watch it back, you watch it back in three different ways. The first time you watch it back, turn up the sound, turn the video and not look at the video, and just listen to yourself. Do an auditory review on how you sound. Do I like the way I sound? Do I like my voice? Do I sound happy? Do I sound sad? Take a whole bunch of notes. Then after that, grab the video, turn... Sound sad? Take a whole bunch of notes. Then after that, grab the video, turn the sound off. Now watch your body language. Now in this moment, I'm talking about something happy I know, but I look neutral. I look sad. What am I doing with my hands? My hands are always by my side. I don't move them at all. I keep gesturing like this all the time. My gesture vocabulary is too limited. Take notes. And then the final step, you get the video transcribed. Plug it into ChatGPT to go, what was the structure of my thinking like? Did I deliver it well? What were my grammatical errors? Take notes and then you improve them the next time you do it.

descripcion:
The fastest way to stop someone from wasting your time is to move the conversation back to action. Stay polite, stay direct, and stop rewarding conversations that go nowhere. 

Comment “pdf” and I’ll send you my free Anger Translator Guide 🤝

#communicationtips #professionalcommunication #workplaceconfidence #emotionalintelligence
transcripcion: how to professionally say, you're wasting my time. One, let's focus on what moves us for. Translation, we're going in circles. Get to the point. Two, what's the next concrete step here? Translation, stop talking. Let's actually do something. Three, if there's nothing new to add, I'm comfortable closing this out. Translation, we're done here. Want more? Comment PDF and I'll send you a link to my free anger translator guide and follow how to convince with lawyer Vince.

bio:
Built and ran the teams nobody saw coming.  Leadership, high-performance, surviving toxic workplace.  SIGN UP FOR THE DARK HORSE NEWSLETTER. 
🔗 Link below 👇
descripcion:
The worst managers in the world aren’t the inexperienced ones.

They’re the ones who can’t let go.

They don’t build leaders. They build minions.

They confuse being feared with being respected.

And the second somebody on their team becomes truly capable, they feel threatened — not proud.

Every one of those failures is a choice.

#leadership #emotionalintelligence #leadershipfail #workplacepsychology #managementtips
transcripcion: You asked me the other day who the worst managers in the world actually are. Well, it's not what you think. It's not the inexperienced ones. It's not the ones without degrees. It's not even the ones who couldn't do the job before they got promoted. The worst managers, in my opinion, are the ones who can't let shit go. They micromanage everything. They think nobody can do the job as well as they can. They spend their highest value time on the lowest value shit because they refuse to trust the people around them. That's not managing, that's bottlenecking. And the entire organization pays the price for their ego. Here's what separates failed managers from the real ones. Failed managers don't build leaders. They build minions. They hire people to follow orders, not to solve the damn problem. They need to be needed. Which means they will never develop somebody who can operate without them. Because the second somebody on their team becomes truly capable, the failed manager feels threatened. Not proud, but threatened. And here's the part that catches people off guard. The worst managers also have zero emotional intelligence. They can't read the damn room. They can't adapt. They run on fear instead of trust. They confuse being feared with being respected. And those are two completely different things. The research is unambiguous. Emotional intelligence predicts management success more reliably than experience or expertise. Every single time. The manager who can connect, adapt, and create an environment Where people feel safe to perform. They win every time. Over the one hiding behind a title and barking orders. Here's the good news. Every one of these failures is actually fixable. Delegate instead of control. Develop leaders instead of followers. Build emotional intelligence instead of hiding behind your authority. The managers who fail aren't dumber than everybody else. They just can't let shit go. They can't develop people, and they can't connect. That's it. That's the whole list. And every one of those things is a choice.

transcripcion: is love enough love the feeling or love the action you told me love the action is because love as an action is willingness willingness to find the color the nuance the balance willingness to learn to love someone how they want to be loved willingness to show up and be loving when you feel the least loving you know people say i would die for my partner i love them more than anything in this world i would literally lay down and die for them okay but would you put your phone in this world i would literally lay down and die for them okay but would you put your phone down when they're telling you about your day do you take their hand when you're walking down the sidewalk with them do you notice when they come home from work and their mood is a little off do you take the time to stop and ask them about it to remember the little things that they tell you to care that's love that's love as an action that's what keeps connection

descripcion: WHAT IF THEY TREAT THE NEXT PERSON BETTER?
transcripcion: But what if they go and treat the next person better? What if he or she gets everything that I've been asking for? If this sounds like you, I want you to stop and consider this. What if you find someone who doesn't make you beg? What if you find someone who hears your needs and feels honored and excited to build that relationship with you? Because the reality is, you won't have to beg the right person. They won't make you feel like the things that you want are unreasonable. And the other reality is, you get to choose who you... are unreasonable and the other reality is you get to choose who you date next and the kind of relationship that you want to create keep your focus on you and what you want forget about the rest of it

descripcion:
More responsibility should come with more clarity. If the expectations keep growing, it’s okay to ask whether your role and compensation are growing too 🤝
#communicationtips #careerstrategy #workplaceconfidence #professionalcommunication
transcripcion: What to say when they want more from you but won't pay more? 1. I'm happy to help in moments of urgency, but if this is an ongoing responsibility, we should align it with my role and pay. Translation, I'll do a favor, but I won't be exploited. 2. I can take this on. Which of my current priorities should I deprioritize to make room? Translation, more work means something else moves down. You decide. 3. I want to be aligned with you. Is there a timeline for a compensation review if these responsibilities become permanent? I'm lying for a compensation review if these responsibilities become permanent. Translation, I'm not ignoring you. I'm simply asking for clarity. Save this video and follow How to Convince with Lawyer Vince.

bio: I’m going on tour! Vote for your city ↓
descripcion:
If you want peace in your life,
then you have to learn to be okay with being misunderstood.
transcripcion: I know one thing for sure, and that's if you want peace in your life, then you have to learn to be okay with being misunderstood. Stop fighting to be seen, heard, or approved of by people who are incapable or simply uninterested in seeing your perspective. People can only meet you and understand you as deeply as they've met and understood themselves. The more you try to change their level of perception, the more peace you give up. So simply... the more peace you give up. learn to let it be.

bio:
Communication and mindset tips from a lawyer 🤝
Free Case Evaluation, PDFs, Newsletter, and Podcast👇 ✉️ contact@howtoconvince.me
descripcion:
Workplace bullies look for reactions, not confidence. Stay calm, set the boundary once, and refuse to make yourself smaller just to keep the peace 🤝
#communicationtips #workplaceconfidence #professionalboundaries #leadershipskills
transcripcion: how to stand up to a workplace bully. One, control your reaction. The reaction is the reward, so take it away. When they come at you, don't match their energy, but don't shrink from it either. Stay level and stay calm. A simple, I hear you and move on. Two, say it once and say it simply. You don't need a speech and you don't need to win. Just say, I'd like to be spoken to differently and then stop talking. You're not trying to embarrass them. You're making one thing clear. This doesn't work on me. Three, stop. making one thing clear. This doesn't work on me. Three, stop shrinking and start standing. Look, you've been working harder, staying quiet or going out of your way. And it's not working because bullies don't respect people who make themselves smaller. They respect people who stay solid. So show up, do your job, hold your ground. That's how you stop being a target. Send this video to a friend and follow How to Convince with Lawyer Vince.

bio: I’m going on tour! Vote for your city ↓
descripcion: DISCERNMENT = EMOTIONAL INTELLIGENCE
transcripcion: One of the truest signs of emotional intelligence is discernment. You may understand exactly why someone does what they do. You see their patterns, their pain, their past. You can connect all the dots and see the bigger picture. But you don't allow your empathy to override your self -respect. Just because you can understand why someone is treating you poorly doesn't mean you have to continue tolerating it. True emotional intelligence doesn't excuse hurtful behavior. It allows you to see someone's complexity. intelligence doesn't excuse hurtful behavior it allows you to see someone's complexities while also discerning what's yours to hold versus what's theirs to heal at the end of the day empathy without boundaries isn't emotional intelligence it isn't love it's self -abandonment bye
